failure: Generated v1 passed parser-level slide/text checks but PowerPoint could not read it.
cause: Deleting existing slides with python-pptx and saving produced duplicate slide XML entries in the pptx package.
lesson: Never accept structure-only checks for PowerPoint. Open the output in desktop PowerPoint before handover. Prefer editing existing template slides or generating a fresh version without delete-and-save package churn.

failure: Quote slide v3 rendered with long quote text overflowing horizontally off the slide.
cause: The chosen template layout did not wrap the long quote safely in the rendered PowerPoint view.
lesson: For quote slides, manually insert line breaks and set explicit text-box widths and font size. Screenshot-check long quotes.

failure: Real Risk slide v4 placed body text over the bright blue bottom wave, reducing contrast.
cause: Template background safe regions were not considered during text placement.
lesson: Visual QA must include background contrast. Move important text away from bright or busy decorative regions.

failure: Attempting to overwrite v2 failed while the deck was open in PowerPoint.
cause: PowerPoint held a file lock on the output deck.
lesson: Use monotonic versions for iterative PowerPoint work and do not overwrite files currently open in Office.

failure: A metric callout rendered across the boundary between two content cards and looked like accidental overlap despite being structurally valid.
cause: The metric box was centered between adjacent cards instead of being visually owned by one card or by a clearly separate full-width band.
lesson: Metric callouts need explicit visual ownership. Place them inside a parent card, align them to a deliberate full-width section, or create enough spacing so they do not straddle card boundaries. Always screenshot-check callout positioning in desktop PowerPoint.

failure: A timeline slide passed file-level checks but rendered with years broken into stacked digits and labels colliding.
cause: Year text boxes were too narrow for the font size, and PowerPoint wrapped digits during rendering.
lesson: For timeline slides, render screenshots and verify every milestone. Use smaller year fonts, wider text boxes, explicit no-wrap settings, and alternating labels with enough horizontal spacing.

failure: A quote box appeared blank in the screenshot although the text existed in the pptx package.
cause: The quote text lived in a separate shape that rendered behind the dark quote rectangle.
lesson: Do not assume text exists means text is visible. For quote/callout blocks, either write the text directly into the visible filled shape or verify z-order in desktop PowerPoint screenshots.

failure: Dark-on-dark quote styling made a slide technically populated but practically unreadable.
cause: Text colour was not checked against the actual rendered panel/background colour.
lesson: Visual QA must include contrast review. On navy panels, use white for primary text and gold only for secondary emphasis.
